title: use conmon for exec
body:
This is a starter PR. Do not merge, it is not complete at all.
depends on: https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pull/2377

The point of pushing here is mostly to get feedback on the design. there is still more work to do:
- [x] --user and --workdir don't currently work and I'm not sure why
- [x] a small tty bug
- [x] all the TODO FIXMES

The following items could be added in follow up PRs:
- [ ] currently, exec sessions create an attach socket similarly to how creates create one, but with sessionID instead of containerID. Right now, there's no global tracking of sessionID, so there exists the opportunity (1/2^128) of there being a duplicate. Are we comfortable with this?
- [ ] I'd like to query the version of conmon in the runtime to make sure we won't crash in a confusing manner. I need to figure out the best way to require this while also prohibiting the conmon versions built from the CRI-O repo that are tagged as 1.9 - 1.14
- [ ] add exit codes to the define package 
- [ ] actually implement remote exec

@haircommander just as an ***FYI***: since before you were here we've been driving toward having all the podman dependencies (for CI testing) installed from upstream packages instead of from source.  This eliminates the headache/errors of updating SHA's in 20-places, and it guarantees we're testing what users will actually be using.  For months, separating and packaging conmon has been the blocker to this work in #1936 and now #3429 also.
@cevich I know you're changing the work flow, but did I miss something in including the new version of conmon for the podman in podman case? It's erroring as if it's using the old version...
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3324) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

> did I miss something in including the new version of conmon for the podman in podman case?

IIRC you updated the `dockerfiles`, that should be all that was needed...Oh!  Yes, @baude setup image-builds from master to fire on those, to prevent building them for every run (wasting 15minutes).  So your dockerfile changes here wouldn't be reflected in CI runs for this PR (since the images were built from master).  Let me check something real quick, I've got an idea for a possible workaround for you...

...okay, yeah, it's set here: https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh#L66

As a workaround, you could modify that line temporarily, to point at an image you manually build and store somewhere.  Not pretty, but will allow you to get feedback in the PR (until you remove the temporary change).

Long-term, incorporating a development-loop around changes to those images, would need to use some tricks.  There's a way to cache files for use across tasks in cirrus.  Perhaps that could be used as part of `test_build_cache_images_task` to produce temporary, test-images.  Then the `verify_test_built_images_task` could pick them up, import, and test with them.  I'll add a card for this work and assign myself.

@haircommander I'm good to merge once you're ready, so just say when.

hey @mheon, the conmonPidFile for exec sessions now lives in the exec bundle path, as such, a deferred cleanup of it happens after it's created, and we shouldn't have the problem https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3595 solves. can you look at the changes and verify I'm correct in thinking so?
I think we're set. We still (potentially) leak if you hit Podman itself with a SIGTERM or similar during the exec, so we don't hit the defer, but otherwise it seems fine.

update, I actually just thought of a way to get this to pass organically, so hold on the manual merge
update, i think I have a way to make in_podman green naturally. hold the force merge

Changes LGTM, but what head nods from @mheon and @vrothberg too, especially if you can't get the tests to green.

Personally, I think once merged, this should sit in upstream for a few weeks before we make a new kit with it and that kit should bump the version from 1.4 to 1.5 or 1.5 to 1.6.
/lgtm
/approve

Thanks for sticking with this one through the endless rebases, @haircommander - it's finally merged.
This also fixed https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3179
